# R2.06 · Workshop — make an codemojex guess submission atomic

> The chapter capstone: fold the five R2 coordination patterns into one working move — admit an codemojex
> order onto the bus atomically across runtimes. `Codemojex.Guesses.submit/3` turns an untrusted request into a
> typed place command, minting a branded `ORD` id at acceptance (six closed errors); the order is then admitted onto
> the `{orders}` queue as one inline Lua script (`EchoMQ.Jobs.enqueue` → `@enqueue`: kind check, EXISTS duplicate
> refusal, HSET the three-field row, ZADD pending) — idempotent on retry, never a torn write, over co-located
> `emq:{orders}:*` keys on one slot. It needs no lock and no Redlock because the script is the serialization. Served
> at /redis-patterns/coordination/workshop.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/coordination/workshop): the end-to-end atomic order admission, folding atomic Lua (R2.01), the lease + `attempts` fence (R2.02), single-Valkey-not-Redlock (R2.03), one-slot-no-torn-write (R2.04), and the `{orders}` hashtag (R2.05). Door to the EchoMQ course.

## Up

- [R2 · Coordination](/redis-patterns/coordination) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
